annotations.label: Dot product (algebraic, coordinates)

anchor.snippet: specified with respect to an orthonormal basis , is defined as

annotations.note: `Matrix.dotProduct v w := ∑ i, v i * w i` is exactly the algebraic coordinate definition.

annotations.label: Dot product in three dimensions

anchor.snippet: For instance, in three-dimensional space

annotations.note: The 3D case isn't a standalone declaration but follows by `vec3_dotProduct`/`Matrix.dotProduct` on `Fin 3 → R`.

annotations.label: Dot product as matrix product

anchor.snippet: the dot product can also be written as a matrix product

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses this via `Matrix.mul_apply`/`mulVec`/`vecMul` which unfold to `dotProduct`, but there is no dedicated row-times-column identity for dot product.

annotations.label: Row-times-column matrix expression

anchor.snippet: Expressing the above example in this way

annotations.note: A worked numerical row-by-column example is not present as a declaration.

annotations.label: Dot product (geometric)

anchor.snippet: The dot product of two Euclidean vectors

annotations.note: `cos_angle_mul_norm_mul_norm` gives `Real.cos (angle x y) * (‖x‖ * ‖y‖) = ⟪x, y⟫`, the geometric definition.

annotations.label: Orthogonal and codirectional cases

anchor.snippet: In particular, if the vectors

annotations.note: The perpendicular case is captured by `inner_eq_zero_iff`/`orthogonal`; the codirectional/antidirectional cases are not stated as such but are direct consequences.

annotations.label: Scalar projection

anchor.snippet: The scalar projection (or scalar component) of a Euclidean vector

annotations.note: Mathlib has `orthogonalProjection` (the vector projection) but no named scalar projection `‖a‖ cos θ`.

annotations.label: Geometric characterization of dot product

anchor.snippet: The dot product is thus characterized geometrically by

annotations.note: `cos_angle_mul_norm_mul_norm` is the inner product = ‖x‖‖y‖cos θ identity.

annotations.label: Homogeneity and distributivity

anchor.snippet: is homogeneous under scaling in each variable

annotations.note: `add_dotProduct`, `dotProduct_add`, `smul_dotProduct`, `dotProduct_smul` (and corresponding inner-product lemmas) give bilinearity.

annotations.label: Dot product is positive-definite bilinear form

anchor.snippet: These properties may be summarized by saying that the dot product is a bilinear form

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace` axiomatizes a positive-definite (sesqui)bilinear form; the real version specializes to bilinear and symmetric.

annotations.label: Standard basis orthonormality and Kronecker delta

anchor.snippet: are the standard basis vectors

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace.basisFun` is an `OrthonormalBasis` and `EuclideanSpace.inner_single_left` evaluates the Kronecker-delta inner product.

annotations.label: Component expansion via geometric dot product

anchor.snippet: Also, by the geometric definition

annotations.note: Component expansion via orthonormal basis is captured by `OrthonormalBasis.sum_repr`/`inner_single_left`, not as a Wikipedia-style scalar component identity.

annotations.label: Equivalence of geometric and algebraic dot products

anchor.snippet: Now applying the distributivity of the geometric version of the dot product

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace.inner_eq_star_dotProduct` identifies the abstract inner product on `EuclideanSpace 𝕜 ι` with the coordinate `dotProduct`.

annotations.label: Associativity with scalar multiplication

anchor.snippet: Because the dot product is not defined between a scalar

annotations.note: `smul_dotProduct` and `dotProduct_smul` give `(c • v) ⬝ᵥ w = c • (v ⬝ᵥ w)`.

annotations.label: Orthogonality criterion

anchor.snippet: Two non-zero vectors

annotations.note: `inner_eq_zero_iff`/`Submodule.orthogonal` characterize orthogonality of vectors via vanishing inner product.

annotations.label: Failure of cancellation law

anchor.snippet: the dot product does not obey the cancellation law

annotations.note: `exists_ne_zero_dotProduct_eq_zero` shows nonzero vectors with zero dot product exist, witnessing the failure of cancellation.

annotations.label: Product rule for dot product of vector-valued functions

anchor.snippet: are vector-valued differentiable functions

annotations.note: `HasDerivAt.inner` (and `deriv_inner_apply`) provide the Leibniz rule for inner products of differentiable maps.

annotations.label: Law of cosines via dot product

anchor.section: Application to the law of cosines
anchor.snippet: which is the law of cosines

annotations.note: This theorem in `Geometry.Euclidean.Triangle` is the vector-form law of cosines via the inner product.

annotations.label: Scalar triple product

anchor.snippet: The scalar triple product of three vectors is defined as

annotations.note: `triple_product_eq_det` states `u ⬝ᵥ v ⨯₃ w = det ![u, v, w]`, defining and identifying the scalar triple product.

annotations.label: Vector triple product (Lagrange's formula)

anchor.snippet: The vector triple product is defined by

annotations.note: `cross_cross` gives the Lie-bracket form of the vector triple product, but the BAC-CAB identity `u × (v × w) = (u·w) v − (u·v) w` is not stated explicitly.

annotations.label: Dot product in physics

anchor.snippet: the dot product takes two vectors and returns a scalar quantity

annotations.note: The physics-motivated narrative description has no formal counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mechanical work

anchor.snippet: Mechanical work is the dot product of force and displacement vectors

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of mechanical work as `F · d`.

annotations.label: Power

anchor.snippet: Power is the dot product of force and velocity

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of physical power `F · v`.

annotations.label: Complex dot product

anchor.snippet: through the alternative definition

annotations.note: The complex Hermitian dot product is the inner product on `EuclideanSpace ℂ ι`, identified with `y ⬝ᵥ star x`.

annotations.label: Sesquilinearity and non-symmetry of complex dot product

anchor.snippet: the complex dot product is sesquilinear rather than bilinear

annotations.note: Sesquilinearity is built into the `InnerProductSpace` axioms (`smul_left`, `conj_inner_symm`).

annotations.label: Angle between complex vectors

anchor.snippet: The angle between two complex vectors is then given by

annotations.note: Mathlib's `InnerProductGeometry.angle` is only defined for real inner product spaces; a complex-vector angle has no named declaration.

annotations.label: Norm squared of complex vector

anchor.snippet: is also known as the norm squared

annotations.note: `norm_sq_eq_re_inner` is part of the `InnerProductSpace` definition: `‖x‖^2 = re ⟪x, x⟫`.

annotations.label: Inner product on abstract vector spaces

anchor.snippet: The inner product generalizes the dot product to abstract vector spaces

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E` is the abstract inner product space generalising the dot product.

annotations.label: Inner product space properties

anchor.snippet: An inner product space is a normed vector space

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace` extends `NormedSpace`; the link `‖x‖^2 = re ⟪x, x⟫` is part of the axioms.

annotations.label: Inner product of square-integrable functions

anchor.snippet: This notion can be generalized to square-integrable functions

annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.L2.innerProductSpace` puts the L2 inner product on `Lp E 2 μ`.

annotations.label: Inner product of continuous real functions

anchor.snippet: For example, if

annotations.note: The example `⟨f, g⟩ = ∫_a^b f g` is not a separate declaration but is a special case of the L2 inner product on `[a,b]`.

annotations.label: Inner product of complex continuous functions

anchor.snippet: Generalized further to complex continuous functions

annotations.note: The complex L2 inner product `∫ conj(f) g` is the `Lp ℂ 2 μ` instance; not stated as a continuous-function example.

annotations.label: Weighted inner product

anchor.snippet: the inner product of functions

annotations.note: Function-space weighted inner products can be obtained via `MeasureTheory.L2` with `μ.withDensity w`; the finite-dimensional weighted form is `Matrix.toInnerProductSpace`, but no dedicated weighted-function-space decl exists.

annotations.label: Frobenius inner product

anchor.snippet: A double-dot product for matrices is the Frobenius inner product

annotations.note: The Frobenius norm/inner product structure exists via `Matrix.frobeniusSeminormedAddCommGroup` (PiLp 2 on entries), but Mathlib lacks a directly named `frobeniusInner` declaration.

annotations.label: Inner product of tensors

anchor.snippet: The inner product between a tensor of order

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.instInnerProductSpace` puts an inner product on `E ⊗[𝕜] F` extending the inner products on the factors.
